evaluation.manager_summary: Father called seeking help for his 24-year-old son struggling with daily heroin use. Agent correctly established consent boundaries but handled the call in a transactional manner, missing opportunities to support the distressed parent, assess urgency (weight loss, visible impairment), and provide concrete next steps or resources. Insurance information was partially collected (no group number), and clinical screening was incomplete.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent acknowledged the caller was scared but failed to build rapport in the first 60 seconds or throughout the call. The tone was transactional and scripted, immediately jumping to procedural questions without acknowledging the difficulty of the situation or the parent's distress.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Start by acknowledging how difficult it is to watch a child struggle with addiction before moving to procedural questions—this builds trust and shows you're listening to the person, not just collecting data.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured carrier and member ID, but did not obtain the group number (caller didn't have it). The agent pivoted to clinical questions without confirming whether verification would happen or exploring alternatives. Functional but minimal.

Agent captured substance type (heroin), frequency (daily use implied), and prior treatment history. However, no exploration of withdrawal symptoms, last use timing, or co-occurring mental health concerns. Stayed within intake boundaries but screening was incomplete.

Agent missed signals that suggest escalating severity (losing weight, falling asleep at dinner, parent concerned enough to call). No assessment of immediacy or exploration of safety concerns. The father's fear was noted but not triaged for urgency.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: When a parent calls about heroin use with signs of escalation (weight loss, impairment), ask about withdrawal risk, safety, and timeframe—this helps determine if we need same-week intervention.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent correctly established consent boundaries early but did so in a legalistic, cold manner that felt like gatekeeping. The father's distress and request for help with the conversation were dismissed rather than addressed. No guidance provided on how to support or approach his son.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: After explaining consent requirements, offer practical support—share tips for approaching a loved one about treatment or offer to send resources that help parents prepare for difficult conversations.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: No cost, fear, or ambivalence objections were raised by the caller. The caller's request for conversation guidance is better scored under family_caller_handling.

Next step was stated (son needs to call), but no specific timing, no offer to schedule a callback window, and no follow-up plan. The father left without clear ownership or a concrete action plan beyond a vague 'have him call when ready.'
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Give the father something concrete—offer to send information he can share, suggest a specific timeframe to follow up, or provide your direct line for when his son is ready to talk.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent appropriately stayed in intake role, avoided diagnostic language, and correctly deferred clinical assessment to the clinical team when discussing MAT. No guarantees or unauthorized clinical statements were made. Clean compliance overall.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. Does your son know you're calling?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I see. Well, since he's an adult, we'd need to talk to him directly before we can move forward with anything.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's understandable. Have him give us a call when he's ready.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, through work. It's CoreFirst Insurance. I have his information here — member ID is BP23737271.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. Do you have a group number?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's okay, we can try to pull it up. Is he using every day?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: He started using about two years ago. It's been heroin. He's been doing well at his job but I can see it's getting worse.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's okay, we can try to pull it up. Is he using every day?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Has he ever tried to stop or get treatment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: He's losing weight. He fell asleep at dinner last week.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's good. I'm just really scared for him.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's understandable. Have him give us a call when he's ready.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I see. Well, since he's an adult, we'd need to talk to him directly before we can move forward with anything.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I understand that. But is there anything you can do to help me prepare for that conversation?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Not really — that's going to depend on him. But if he's willing to call, we can get things moving quickly.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Not really — that's going to depend on him. But if he's willing to call, we can get things moving quickly.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's understandable. Have him give us a call when he's ready.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: He'll need to call himself or come in for an assessment. We can't hold a spot without him being involved.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, we offer medication-assisted treatment. Our clinical team will assess whether that's right for him.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Has he ever tried to stop or get treatment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I see. Well, since he's an adult, we'd need to talk to him directly before we can move forward with anything.
